skill.name: yaml-authoring
skill.description: Create and validate YAML diagram files for cloud architecture diagrams, including nodes, edges, layouts, and common error checks.
skill.summary: Guides YAML authoring for cloud architecture diagrams with validation rules and examples.
skill.author: 7nohe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown examples and cloud service names. The only confirmed risk factor is documented local CLI execution for validation and serving diagrams, with no evidence of malicious intent, exfiltration, obfuscation, prompt injection, or unsafe dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Documented Local CLI Commands
low_findings.description: The skill includes bash examples that run a local Bun CLI to build or serve a YAML diagram. This is a real external command pattern, but the commands are explicit documentation examples and do not include dynamic shell construction, network exfiltration, credential access, or hidden execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly visible in the validation section and use fixed arguments. Confidence is medium-high because execution depends on the user choosing to run the documented commands.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The C2, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick findings are explained by Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, YAML field names, EC2 service names, and error examples. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, or Ruby execution logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens appear inside documentation, YAML examples, Markdown tables, and error messages. The file contains no executable Ruby code, malware instructions, or hidden control flow.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent YAML diagram files that validate before diagrams are rendered. This skill explains the schema, layout rules, examples, and common fixes for cloud architecture diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required YAML document fields for diagram files.
Shows how to define nodes, edges, labels, providers, and kinds.
Documents layout rules for top-level nodes and nested child nodes.
Provides examples for Lambda, S3, API Gateway, DynamoDB, VPC, and subnet diagrams.
Lists common validation errors and the likely YAML fixes.
Documents local CLI commands for building and serving diagrams.
limitations: It does not provide a standalone parser or renderer.
It focuses on documented diagram YAML, not every YAML dialect.
It assumes the referenced project CLI is available for validation.
It does not verify cloud account resources or live infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Cloud Diagram
use_cases.description: Create a valid diagram file with required fields, service nodes, edges, labels, and layout values.

use_cases.title: Fix YAML Validation Errors
use_cases.description: Map common parser and schema errors to direct corrections in document structure, IDs, edges, and layout.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture Patterns
use_cases.description: Use consistent examples for APIs, storage workflows, and nested VPC resources in project documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small cloud diagram.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a YAML diagram for one AWS Lambda function that reads from one S3 bucket. Include valid IDs, labels, layout, and one edge.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Validation Error
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an error from the diagram validator.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my YAML diagram error and explain the smallest fix. Focus on required fields, duplicate IDs, edge references, and layout rules.

prompt_templates.title: Add Nested VPC Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate diagram with parent and child nodes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this diagram plan to include a VPC, subnet, load balancer, and Lambda function. Use parent references and valid container dimensions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before validation and rendering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this YAML diagram for schema completeness, stable IDs, layout consistency, parent references, edge targets, and clear labels. Return prioritized fixes.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a Lambda to S3 diagram.
output_examples.output: A complete diagram plan with one Lambda node, one S3 node, clear labels, fixed coordinates, and one directional edge.

output_examples.input: A user reports a missing layout error.
output_examples.output: A short diagnosis explaining that top-level nodes need layout coordinates, while child nodes can use automatic positioning.

output_examples.input: A user wants a VPC architecture diagram.
output_examples.output: A nested structure with a VPC container, subnet container, child services, and edges that reference existing node IDs.

best_practices: Use descriptive, unique IDs for every node and edge.
Give top-level nodes explicit x and y layout values.
Validate parent references and edge targets before rendering.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse the same ID across nodes or edges.
Do not provide only one coordinate when layout needs both x and y.
Do not reference parent nodes or edge targets that do not exist.

faq.question: What kind of YAML does this skill help write?
faq.answer: It helps write YAML files for cloud architecture diagrams with nodes, edges, layout, labels, and service kinds.

faq.question: Can child nodes omit layout values?
faq.answer: Yes. Child nodes with a parent can omit layout values and use automatic positioning.

faq.question: When are width and height required?
faq.answer: Container nodes such as VPCs and subnets require width and height values in their layout.

faq.question: Does this skill run validation by itself?
faq.answer: No. It documents the project CLI commands that a user can run in the appropriate repository.

faq.question: Can it support providers beyond AWS?
faq.answer: The examples focus on AWS, but the provider and kind fields are documented as flexible strings.

faq.question: Does it inspect live cloud infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. It only guides diagram YAML authoring and validation concepts.
